what_it_does: This file is a ledger-style attachment record that links an attachment identifier to a channel and a specific board item target, with a timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter only; no body content
standouts: Target uses a board:// URI scheme pointing to item-93afd7b0
Precise UTC timestamp with microsecond offset
concerns: No descriptive body or context beyond metadata
The board:// target cannot be resolved from this file alone
attachment_type value is not further defined here
notes: A minimal, timestamped metadata stub that attaches a provider-registry channel record to a board item via a board:// URI.
questions: What does board item item-93afd7b0 represent?
What is the semantic meaning of the provider-registry channel?
Is this attachment generated automatically or authored manually?
What does attachment_type board_items imply about the relationship?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a — no stated purpose in the file; the metadata accurately records an attachment reference.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to record that a board item is attached to the provider-registry channel and to preserve when that linkage was made.
novelty: The use of a board:// URI as a target reference in an otherwise plain YAML attachment record.
contribution: It contributes the specific attachment-to-board-item mapping and its recorded time for the provider-registry channel, which a fused system would keep as a provenance link.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record with id att-abea10ab, channel provider-registry, attachment_type board_items, and target board://item-93afd7b0, added on 2026-06-22T15:53:07.330672+00:00.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to record and timestamp a board-item attachment association within the provider-registry channel.
